Section Title: ... > Description
Content:
[](https://github.com/sam7eer)
[sam7eer](https://github.com/sam7eer)
opened [on Mar 3, 2026](https://github.com/vercel/ai/issues/13017)
Issue body actions
When using the gemini-2.5-flash-lite model with the Vercel AI SDK (generateText) and tools enabled, the model exhibits a critical failure during the multi-step maxSteps tool loop.
Specifically, the model successfully identifies the need to call a tool and halts execution correctly in Step 1 (finishReason: 'tool-calls'). However, once the tool executes and passes the exact results back into the model for synthesis (Step 2), the model completely fails to generate the final text response. It immediately terminates with finishReason: 'stop' without returning a single text token.
This issue is exclusively isolated to gemini-2.5-flash-lite. Running the exact same code with gemini-2.5-flash resolves perfectly, successfully synthesizing the tool results into the final output.
Code:
Section Title: ... > Description
Content:
```
import { generateText, tool } from 'ai';
import { google } from '@ai-sdk/google';
import { z } from 'zod';
async function run() {
  const { text, steps } = await generateText({
    // Fails on Lite. Changing this to 'gemini-2.5-flash' completely fixes the bug. model: google('gemini-2.5-flash-lite'),
    system: 'You are a helpful assistant.
 ...

Content:
[ai/provider related to a provider package. Must be assigned together with at least one `provider/\*` label](https://github.com/vercel/ai/issues?q=state%3Aopen%20label%3A%22ai%2Fprovider%22) related to a provider package.
 ... 
Section Title: `@ai-sdk/google{-vertex}` : ignores `strict:true` on tools \
Content:
[](https://github.com/heiwen)
 ... 
Section Title: `@ai-sdk/google{-vertex}` : ignores `strict:true` on tools \ > Description > Description
Content:
In the AI SDK Google and Google Vertex providers, tool strictness is not reflected in Gemini function-calling mode.
From an end-user perspective, setting `strict: true` on tools should enable validated/strict function calling behavior, but today the provider sends `ANY` (or `AUTO` / `NONE` ) only.
**Minimal Reproduction**
Section Title: `@ai-sdk/google{-vertex}` : ignores `strict:true` on tools \ > Description > Description
Content:
```
import { generateText , tool } from 'ai' ; 
 import { google } from '@ai-sdk/google' ; 
 import { z } from 'zod' ; 

 await generateText ( { 
  model : google ( 'gemini-3-flash-preview' ) , 
  toolChoice : 'required' , 
  tools : { 
    createMeeting : tool ( { 
      description : 'Create a meeting' , 
      inputSchema : z . object ( { 
        title : z . string ( ) , 
        startTime : z . string ( ) , 
        attendees : z . array ( z . string ( ) ) , 
      } ) , 
      strict : true , // ignored 
    } ) , 
  } , 
  prompt : 'Schedule a product review tomorrow with alice@example.com' , 
 } ) ;
```
**Expected**
When tool strictness is requested ( `strict: true` ), Gemini should use validated function calling mode ( `VALIDATED` ).
[https://ai.google.dev/gemini-api/docs/function-calling_calling_modes](https://ai.google.dev/gemini-api/docs/function-calling)
Section Title: `@ai-sdk/google{-vertex}` : ignores `strict:true` on tools \ > Description > Description
Content:
**Actual**
Google provider currently maps function calling config modes to `AUTO | NONE | ANY` only, so validated mode is not used.
**Impact**
Less predictable schema-conformant tool arguments
Extra app-side validation/retry logic
Inconsistent strict behavior across providers
**Request**
Please add support in the Google/Gemini provider to use `VALIDATED` mode when strict tool behavior is requested (e.g. `strict: true` ), and keep existing behavior for non-strict tools.
 ... 
Section Title: `@ai-sdk/google{-vertex}` : ignores `strict:true` on tools \ > Metadata > Labels
Content:
[ai/provider related to a provider package. Must be assigned together with at least one `provider/\*` label](https://github.com/vercel/ai/issues?q=state%3Aopen%20label%3A%22ai%2Fprovider%22) related to a provider package.

results.title: AI SDK 6 - Vercel
excerpts:
Section Title: AI SDK 6 > Link to heading Agents > Link to heading Call Options
Content:
With call options, you can pass type-safe arguments when you call `generate` or `stream` on a ToolLoopAgent. For example, you can use them to inject retrieved documents for RAG, select models based on request complexity, or customize tool behavior per request.
 ... 
Section Title: AI SDK 6 > Link to heading Tool Improvements > Link to heading Input Examples
Content:
Input examples are currently only natively supported by Anthropic. For providers that don't support them, you can use [`addToolInputExamplesMiddleware`](https://ai-sdk.dev/docs/ai-sdk-core/middleware) to append the examples to the tool description. If no middleware is used and the provider doesn't support input examples, they are ignored and not sent to the provider.
 ... 
Section Title: AI SDK 6 > Link to heading Tool Improvements > Link to heading Send Custom Tool Output to the Model
Content:
To learn more, check out the [Tool Calling documentation](https://ai-sdk.dev/docs/ai-sdk-core/tools-and-tool-calling) .
 ... 
Section Title: AI SDK 6 > Link to heading Provider Tools > Link to heading Google Provider Tools
Content:
Google Maps Tool: Enable location-aware responses with Maps grounding (Gemini 2.0+)
Vertex RAG Store Tool: Retrieve context from Vertex AI RAG Engine corpora (Gemini 2.0+)
File Search Tool: Semantic and keyword search in file search stores (Gemini 2.5+)
Section Title: AI SDK 6 > Link to heading Provider Tools > Link to heading Google Provider Tools
Content:
```
1

import { google } from "@ai-sdk/google" ; 2 3

// Google Maps Tool - location-aware grounding 4

const googleMaps = google . tools . googleMaps ( ) ; 5 6

// Vertex RAG Store Tool - retrieve from RAG corpora 7

const vertexRagStore = google . tools . vertexRagStore ( { 8

ragCorpus : "projects/{project}/locations/{location}/ragCorpora/{rag_corpus}" , 9

topK : 5 , // optional: number of contexts to retrieve 10

} ) ; 11 12

// File Search Tool - search in file stores 13

const fileSearch = google . tools . fileSearch ( { 14

fileSearchStoreNames : [ "fileSearchStores/my-store-123" ] , 15

topK : 10 , // optional: number of chunks to retrieve 16

metadataFilter : "author=John Doe" , // optional: AIP-160 filter 17

} ) ;
```
To learn more, check out [the Google documentation](https://ai-sdk.dev/providers/ai-sdk-providers/google-generative-ai) .

I’m encountering an issue when using the `@ai-sdk/google` integration in the Vercel AI SDK with the `gemini-2.0-flash-001` model. I’m trying to use the `streamText` function along with tools and the `experimental_output` option.
code:
Section Title: Error with @ai-sdk/google using streamText
Content:
```
import { createGoogleGenerativeAI } from "@ai-sdk/google";
import { Output, smoothStream, streamText, tool } from "ai";
import { z } from "zod";

const model = createGoogleGenerativeAI({
  apiKey: EncodedVideoChunk.GEMINI_API_KEY,
});

const Schema = z.object({
  response: z.string(),
});

const getWeatherInformation = tool({
  description: "show the weather in a given city to the user",
  parameters: z.object({ city: z.string() }),
  execute: async ({ city }) => {
    console.log(`Getting weather information for ${city}`);
    return `The weather in ${city} is sunny`;
  },
});

export const tools = {
  getWeatherInformation,
};

const result = streamText({
  model: model("gemini-2.0-flash-001"),
  messages: [
    { role: "system", content: "You are a helpful assistant." },
    { role: "user", content: "Give me weather of gujarat" },
  ],
  tools,
  maxSteps: 2,
  onError: (error) => {
    console.error("Error while streaming:",
 ... 
Section Title: Error with @ai-sdk/google using streamText
Content:
However, I’m consistently getting the following error:
```
{
  "error": {
    "code": 400,
    "message": "For controlled generation of only function calls (forced function calling), please set 'tool_config.function_calling_config.mode' field to ANY instead of populating 'response_mime_type' and 'response_schema' fields. For more details, see: https://cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/generative-ai/docs/multimodal/function-calling",
    "status": "INVALID_ARGUMENT"
  }
}
```
Interestingly, when I use the OpenAI integration with a similar setup (streaming text with tools), it works without any issues.
Could you please help me understand what might be causing this error with the Google Gemini model and how I can resolve it? It seems like there might be a specific configuration requirement for Gemini when using tools and `experimental_output` with `streamText` that I’m missing.
Any guidance or suggestions would be greatly appreciated.
Thanks!
​

AI SDK Core Tool Calling
Copy markdown
Section Title: Tool Calling
Content:
As covered under Foundations, tools are objects that can be called by the model to perform a specific task.
AI SDK Core tools contain several core elements:
**`description`** : An optional description of the tool that can influence when the tool is picked.
**`inputSchema`** : A Zod schema or a JSON schema that defines the input parameters. The schema is consumed by the LLM, and also used to validate the LLM tool calls.
**`execute`** : An optional async function that is called with the inputs from the tool call. It produces a value of type `RESULT` (generic type). It is optional because you might want to forward tool calls to the client or to a queue instead of executing them in the same process.
**`strict`** : *(optional, boolean)* Enables strict tool calling when supported by the provider
You can use the `tool` helper function to infer the types of the `execute` parameters.
 ... 
Section Title: Tool Calling > Multi-Step Calls (using stopWhen) > `prepareStep` callback
Content:
```
1

import { generateText } from 'ai' ; 2 3

const result = await generateText ( { 4

// ... 5

prepareStep : async ( { model , stepNumber , steps , messages } ) => { 6

if ( stepNumber === 0 ) { 7

return { 8

// use a different model for this step: 9

model : modelForThisParticularStep , 10

// force a tool choice for this step: 11

toolChoice : { type : 'tool' , toolName : 'tool1' } , 12

// limit the tools that are available for this step: 13

activeTools : [ 'tool1' ] , 14

} ; 15

} 16 17

// when nothing is returned, the default settings are used 18

} , 19

} ) ;
```
 ... 
Section Title: Tool Calling > Tool Choice
Content:
You can use the `toolChoice` setting to influence when a tool is selected.
It supports the following settings:
`auto` (default): the model can choose whether and which tools to call.
`required` : the model must call a tool. It can choose which tool to call.
`none` : the model must not call tools
`{ type: 'tool', toolName: string (typed) }` : the model must call the specified tool
Gateway Provider Custom
Claude Sonnet 4.5
 ... 
Section Title: Tool Calling > Multi-modal Tool Results
Content:
Multi-modal tool results are experimental and supported by Anthropic, OpenAI, and Google (Gemini 3 models).
For Google, use base64 media parts ( `image-data` / `file-data` ) or base64 `data:` URLs in URL-style parts. Remote HTTP(S) URLs in tool-result URL parts
are not supported.
In order to send multi-modal tool results, e.g. screenshots, back to the model,
they need to be converted into a specific format.
AI SDK Core tools have an optional `toModelOutput` function
that converts the tool result into a content part.
Here is an example for converting a screenshot into a content part:
Gateway Provider Custom
Claude Sonnet 4.5

results.title: Vercel AI SDK returns empty text after MCP tool call resolution
excerpts:
[▲ Vercel Community](/) · [Categories](/categories) · [Latest](/latest) · [Top](/top) · [Live](/live)
[AI SDK](/c/ai-sdk/62)
Section Title: Vercel AI SDK returns empty text after MCP tool call resolution
Content:
710 views · 0 likes · 2 posts
Vineetchotaliya 6907 (@vineetchotaliya-6907) · 2025-06-25
When invoking a tool via `experimental_createMCPClient()` and `generateText` using the Vercel AI SDK, the final `.text` field in the response is always an empty string, even though the tool result contains valid data.
Issue replicates even with `maxToolRoundtrips: 3` set.
```
"text": "",
  "files": [],
  "reasoningDetails": [],
  "toolCalls": [
    {
      "type": "tool-call",
      "toolCallId": "9COeKo3dUxyMgLea",
      "toolName": "get_l1_orderbook",
...
```
FYI Env
```
ai: 4.3.16

@ai-sdk/google: 1.2.19

bun: 1.2.16

Model: gemini-1.5-pro-latest

Tools: experimental_createMCPClient() (SSE-based)
```
Jacob Paris (@jacobparis) · 2025-06-26
Tool calls store their output in the tool result and won't usually have a .text field, unless you've explicitly requested it like "get the weather for London and return a summary"

results.title: Google Generative AI Provider - AI SDK
excerpts:
[](https://vercel.com/oss)
Docs
Resources
[AI Gateway Gateway](https://vercel.com/ai-gateway)
Menu
AI SDK Providers Google Generative AI
Copy markdown
 ... 
Section Title: Google Generative AI Provider > Setup
Content:
The Google provider is available in the `@ai-sdk/google` module. You can install it with
pnpm npm yarn bun
```
pnpm add @ai-sdk/google
```
 ... 
Section Title: Google Generative AI Provider > Provider Instance
Content:
**baseURL** *string*Use a different URL prefix for API calls, e.g. to use proxy servers.
The default prefix is `https://generativelanguage.googleapis.com/v1beta` .
**apiKey** *string*API key that is being sent using the `x-goog-api-key` header.
It defaults to the `GOOGLE_GENERATIVE_AI_API_KEY` environment variable.
**headers** *Record<string,string>*Custom headers to include in the requests.
**fetch** *(input: RequestInfo, init?: RequestInit) => Promise<Response>*Custom [fetch](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/fetch) implementation.
Defaults to the global `fetch` function.
You can use it as a middleware to intercept requests,
or to provide a custom fetch implementation for e.g. testing.
**generateId** *() => string*Optional function to generate unique IDs for each request.
Defaults to the SDK's built-in ID generator.
**name** *string*Custom provider name.
Defaults to `'google.generative-ai'` .
Section Title: Google Generative AI Provider > Language Models
Content:
You can create models that call the [Google Generative AI API](https://ai.google.dev/api/rest) using the provider instance.
The first argument is the model id, e.g. `gemini-2.5-flash` .
The models support tool calls and some have multi-modal capabilities.
```
1

const model = google ( 'gemini-2.5-flash' ) ;
```
You can use Google Generative AI language models to generate text with the `generateText` function:
```
1

import { google } from '@ai-sdk/google' ; 2

import { generateText } from 'ai' ; 3 4

const { text } = await generateText ( { 5

model : google ( 'gemini-2.5-flash' ) , 6

prompt : 'Write a vegetarian lasagna recipe for 4 people.' , 7

} ) ;
```
Google Generative AI language models can also be used in the `streamText` function
and support structured data generation with `Output` (see AI SDK Core ).
 ... 
Section Title: Google Generative AI Provider > Image Models
Content:
You can create image models that call the Google Generative AI API using the `.image()` factory method.
For more on image generation with the AI SDK see generateImage() .
The Google provider supports two types of image models:
**Imagen models** : Dedicated image generation models using the `:predict` API
**Gemini image models** : Multimodal language models with image output capabilities using the `:generateContent` API
 ... 
Section Title: Google Generative AI Provider > Image Models > Gemini Image Models
Content:
[Gemini image models](https://ai.google.dev/gemini-api/docs/image-generation) (e.g. `gemini-2.5-flash-image` ) are technically multimodal output language models, but they can be used with the `generateImage()` function for a simpler image generation experience. Internally, the provider calls the language model API with `responseModalities: ['IMAGE']` .
```
1

import { google } from '@ai-sdk/google' ; 2

import { generateImage } from 'ai' ; 3 4

const { image } = await generateImage ( { 5

model : google . image ( 'gemini-2.5-flash-image' ) , 6

prompt : 'A photorealistic image of a cat wearing a wizard hat' , 7

aspectRatio : '1:1' , 8

} ) ;
```
Gemini image models also support image editing by providing input images:

Section Title: ... > DexterW1 Aug 21, 2025
Content:
-
|Hi! I’m running into an issue with ai-sdk v4 using streamText with the Google provider.
```
const result = await streamText({
    model: google('gemini-2.5-flash'),
    system: promptSelector(promptStyle)
    messages,
    tools: getToolsForPrompt(promptStyle),
    maxSteps: 2,
    onStepFinish: (step) => {
      // mask request body in logs
      const { request, ...rest } = step
      const safeStep = {
        ...rest,
        request: request ? { ...request, body: '[omitted]' } : undefined,
      }
      console.log('Step finished:', JSON.stringify(safeStep, null, 2))
    },
    onError: (error) => {
      console.error('Error while streaming:', JSON.stringify(error, null, 2))
    },
  })
```
On the first step, I consistently get a step-start event, then it stops with finishReason: "error". No tool is executed, and the assistant never produces text. If I send the same message a couple of times it will work.
